forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: A slight chance of rain after 4am. Mostly clear, with a low around 54. South wind around 12 mph, with gusts as high as 20 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

forecast.name: Monday
forecast.shortForecast: Light Rain Likely then Showers And Thunderstorms
forecast.detailedForecast: Rain likely before 2pm, then showers and thunderstorms between 2pm and 5pm, then rain. Mostly cloudy. High near 64, with temperatures falling to around 60 in the afternoon. South southwest wind 10 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph. Chance of precipitation is 90%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.

weather_report:
In the heart of Downtown San Francisco, the night brings a gentle chill, with a low settling around 54 degrees, a familiar whisper of moisture in the air. As dawn creeps closer, the sky remains mostly clear, yet the promise of rain lingers just beyond the horizon, poised to awaken the city after its slumber. The south winds beckon softly, rustling through the promise of spring, hinting at what the day might unveil.

As Monday unfolds, drapes of grey will envelop the skyline, thickening as the sun makes its ascent, giving way to rain likely before mid-afternoon. Expect showers and the occasional rumble of thunder to weave through the day, with temperatures retreating to the mid-60s, a latent coolness wrapping itself around you. A brisk south-southwest wind will accompany the moisture, lending an invigorating edge to the dreariness. With a near-certain 90% chance of precipitation, donning a waterproof jacket or carrying an umbrella will be essential for any foray outside. Today is one for reflection, perhaps a perfect backdrop for a stroll through the galleries of Chinatown or a quiet visit to a cozy café, warm drink in hand, as the streets transform under the embrace of spring rain.
